domain: Maori haka collective stamped synchrony (foot-stamping waewae takahia + rhythmically shouted accompaniment + pukana facial expression; each performer linked in step; out-of-sync = bad omen)
specific_mechanism: At every K-th attention layer (or every K-th token), inject a phase-reset anchor that hard-projects every attention head's current RoPE rotation phase onto a SHARED reference rotor — re-grounding inter-head phase coherence collectively. Between anchors heads evolve independently; at each anchor a multi-head rotor-Stiefel projection forces phase realignment to the common reference.
llm_application: HAKA-PHASE-LOCK: a periodic phase-reset mechanism for multi-head RoPE attention. At fixed positions (every K tokens or every L layers) inject a shared-rotor anchor that hard-projects each head's current rotation phase onto a common reference rotor R0(t). Concretely, for head h at layer l within anchor interval, apply Q_h, K_h := Π_{R0(t)}(Q_h R_h(l)), Π_{R0(t)}(K_h R_h(l)) where Π is orthogonal projection onto the affine subspace defined by R0(t). Anchor positions act as 'foot-stamps' that re-ground inter-head phase coherence. Differs from PEPE (period-aware extrapolation, not hard reset to shared rotor), Phase-Aligned RoPE CRPA 2511.19778 (stride-aligned phase mapping, not periodic reset), and SyncLLM (synchronization tokens for chunk interleaving, not RoPE phase). Differs from R421 DERVISH-RING (continuous activation rotation steering) by being PERIODIC-EVENT phase-reset specifically of RoPE rotation, not continuous steering.
novelty_claim: PEPE + Phase-Aligned RoPE + SyncLLM + Preplan-and-Anchor cover periodic RoPE phase and anchor-token synchronization. HAKA-PHASE-LOCK adds the SHARED-ROTOR multi-head projection at periodic anchors. Recombination.
